content:
# Pyrene

**Summary**: A tetracyclic aromatic hydrocarbon, often used as a model for high molecular weight PAHs.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2015-malik|Malik, 2015]] investigated the degradation of pyrene by various strains.

- [[2017-budiyanto|Budiyanto, 2017]] investigated the degradation of pyrene by halophilic bacteria.

## Scientific Context

- [[2015-malik|Malik, 2015]] pyrene is a highly persistent PAH.

- [[2017-budiyanto|Budiyanto, 2017]] investigated the degradation of pyrene by halophilic bacteria.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2015-malik|Malik, 2015]] measured by concentration reduction over 18 days.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Pyrene was more resistant to biodegradation than naphthalene or phenanthrene. ([[2015-malik|Malik, 2015, p. 95]])


- The fastest degradation rate was observed in Halomonas smyrnensis 20PY1A at 37 °C and pH 7. ([[2017-budiyanto|Budiyanto, 2017, p. 20]])
